title: The Century Report: March 14 2026
description:
Capital alone is proven insufficient in AI development

Meta is delaying its flagship "Avocado" AI model after internal tests showed it trailing Google, OpenAI, and Anthropic - and has reportedly discussed licensing Gemini to power its own products - while nine of xAI's twelve cofounders have now departed and Musk has ordered a rebuild from the foundations, with both organizations discovering that frontier AI capability cannot be purchased through capital deployment alone. Wired published the most detailed public account yet of how Palantir's Maven integrates AI into military targeting chains, from satellite imagery through target nomination to bombardment asset assignment - arriving the same day a Lancet Psychiatry review documented three categories of AI-associated delusions and found that only Anthropic's Claude consistently refused to assist users in planning violent attacks. The DOE announced $1.9 billion for transmission reconductoring to double capacity on existing lines without new construction, extending the infrastructure logic PJM validated yesterday with ambient-adjusted ratings, while Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory identified 2.3 million regulatory DNA sequences conserved for over 400 million years of plant evolution, and Cambridge scientists discovered a light-powered late-stage drug modification reaction published in Nature Synthesis.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-14-2026/
